compiled_state.title: Ticket system validation and classification layer: validate, classify, quality gates
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Context

Seven deterministic ticket validation and classification scripts live outside the ticket system despite performing pure ticket operations — checking ticket health, scoring clarity, validating acceptance criteria, assessing dispatch readiness, classifying tasks for agent routing, generating summaries, and purging stale bridge data. Each script already uses the ticket CLI for data access but is not part of the ticket system itself. Absorbing them makes the ticket system opinionated — able to answer "is this ticket ready for dispatch?" and "how should this task be routed?" — rather than being a passive data store that external scripts interrogate.

## Success Criteria

- Seven new ticket CLI subcommands exist and are callable via the ticket CLI dispatcher: validate (health diagnostics with --quick/--full/--fix/--json/--terse flags and 5-tier scored exit codes 0-4 — replaces validate-issues.sh), classify (deterministic keyword scoring accepting ticket ID, outputting JSON with model/subagent/class/complexity/priority fields — replaces classify-task.sh/py), clarity-check (heuristic clarity scoring — replaces ticket-clarity-check.sh), check-ac (acceptance criteria block validation — replaces check-acceptance-criteria.sh), quality-check (readiness validation for issue-as-prompt dispatch — replaces issue-quality-check.sh), summary (one-line ticket summary with blocking status for one or more ticket IDs — replaces issue-summary.sh), and purge-bridge (removal of inbound bridge tickets not matching a specified project key — replaces purge-non-project-tickets.sh)
- Each new subcommand has an integration test in tests/ exercising the subcommand through the ticket CLI dispatcher against a synthetic fixture, asserting output equivalence (same exit codes, same JSON keys and values, same text format) with the script it replaces
- classify uses the existing deterministic keyword-matching logic from classify-task.py (no LLM); the poetry environment resolution requirement is documented in the subcommand's implementation

## Done Definitions

- When this epic is complete, developers can validate ticket health via `ticket validate` with --quick/--full/--fix/--json/--terse flags and the same 5-tier scored exit codes (0-4) as validate-issues.sh
  ← Satisfies: "validate (health diagnostics with --quick/--full/--fix/--json/--terse flags and 5-tier scored exit codes 0-4)"
- When this epic is complete, developers can classify tasks via `ticket classify <id>` and receive JSON with model/subagent/class/complexity/priority fields using the deterministic keyword scoring engine from classify-task.py (no LLM)
  ← Satisfies: "classify (deterministic keyword scoring accepting ticket ID, outputting JSON with model/subagent/class/complexity/priority fields)"
- When this epic is complete, the ticket system answers "is this ticket ready for dispatch?" (quality-check), "is the AC block valid?" (check-ac), and "how should this task be routed?" (classify) through its own CLI — callers no longer invoke separate scripts for these questions
  ← Satisfies: "seven new ticket CLI subcommands exist and are callable via the ticket CLI dispatcher"
- When this epic is complete, each of the seven new subcommands has an integration test asserting output equivalence (same exit codes, JSON keys/values, text format) with the script it replaces
  ← Satisfies: "Each new subcommand has an integration test in tests/ exercising the subcommand through the ticket CLI dispatcher against a synthetic fixture, asserting output equivalence"

## Scope

- IN: Seven ticket-*.sh/py subcommands, dispatcher integration, integration tests against synthetic fixtures, poetry environment resolution documented for classify, preservation of validate's 5-tier exit-code contract and full flag surface
- OUT: Refactoring callers of the absorbed scripts to use the new CLI (caller migration is Epic e1b2-2653's concern); removing or deprecating the original scripts (they remain as thin wrappers if still called anywhere); any LLM-based classification

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Seven new ticket-*.sh/py scripts follow the existing ticket dispatcher pattern — consistent with sibling subcommands
- [Reliability] validate MUST preserve the full --quick/--full/--fix/--json/--terse flag surface and the 5-tier scored exit code contract (0=OK, 4=critical) — downstream callers branch on exit code
- [Reliability] classify MUST preserve classify-task.py's deterministic keyword scoring — no LLM, no behavior change, only a CLI wrapper change
- [Reliability] purge-bridge is LOCAL-ONLY (no Jira API calls) — MUST validate tracker is mounted before any deletion to prevent destructive operation against an unmounted tracker
- [Testing] Each subcommand requires integration test via CLI dispatcher against synthetic fixture; output equivalence with the replaced script is the pass condition
- [Maintainability] classify's poetry environment resolution (for the Python scoring engine) must be documented in the subcommand implementation so callers understand the environment requirement

## Dependencies

None (purely additive — no existing scripts are removed or refactored in this epic; can proceed in parallel with Epic 2c28-e5b9)

## Approach

One ticket-*.sh or ticket-*.py script per subcommand, dispatched from ticket-cli.sh. classify wraps classify-task.py's scoring engine. validate preserves the full --quick/--full/--fix/--json/--terse flag surface and 5-tier exit code contract. purge-bridge performs local-only cleanup (no Jira API calls) — validates tracker is mounted before any deletion.
